framework.reasoning: This is a quantum-computing paper, not a classical deep-learning paper, so the SIR's architecture graph is a set of parametrised quantum circuits (Qiskit QuantumCircuit objects), not a neural network. Qiskit is the mandatory quantum-circuit / hardware-execution backend (the paper cites Qiskit Statevector/Aer and IBM Quantum backends explicitly). PyTorch is selected as the SIR schema's 'primary framework' field to host the classical optimisation loop that trains the QCBM and CRCA variational parameters (gradient-based minimisation of the cross-entropy and MSE losses in the SIR's mathematical_spec), using Qiskit's TorchConnector / parameter-shift or SPSA gradients as the bridge. No CUDA is required since all circuits are small (<=9 qubits) and run on CPU simulators or the IBM Quantum cloud API. YAML is used for configuration (no need for Hydra/OmegaConf given the flat, well-bounded hyperparameter space).

module_hierarchy.description: QCBM (Quantum Circuit Born Machine) state-preparation ansatz G_theta that encodes the joint time-market probability tensor P_{i,j}

public_methods.name: build_circuit
public_methods.signature: build_circuit(self, num_time_qubits: int, num_asset_qubits: int, num_layers: int) -> QuantumCircuit
public_methods.returns: parametrised QuantumCircuit with RX/RZ/RZZ layers

public_methods.name: born_distribution
public_methods.signature: born_distribution(self, params: np.ndarray, backend: str = 'statevector') -> np.ndarray
public_methods.returns: probability array P_theta(x) over 2^(m+n) basis states

public_methods.name: cross_entropy_loss
public_methods.signature: cross_entropy_loss(self, params: np.ndarray, target_dist: np.ndarray, eps_num: float = 1e-8) -> float
public_methods.returns: scalar cross-entropy loss L_QCBM(theta)

public_methods.name: kl_divergence
public_methods.signature: kl_divergence(self, params: np.ndarray, target_dist: np.ndarray, eps_num: float = 1e-8) -> float
public_methods.returns: scalar KL_epsilon diagnostic (not used for training)

module_hierarchy.description: Controlled Rotation Circuit Ansatz (CRCA) blocks for exposure (R_v), discount (R_p) and default-probability (R_q) encoding

public_methods.name: build_circuit
public_methods.signature: build_circuit(self, num_time_qubits: int, num_layers: int) -> QuantumCircuit
public_methods.returns: 3-node-path CRCA circuit for R_p or R_q

public_methods.name: conditional_probability
public_methods.signature: conditional_probability(self, params: np.ndarray, index: int) -> float
public_methods.returns: F_phi(i), the ancilla |1> probability conditioned on time index i

public_methods.name: mse_loss
public_methods.signature: mse_loss(self, params: np.ndarray, target_fn: np.ndarray) -> float
public_methods.returns: scalar MSE loss L_f(phi)

public_methods.name: build_circuit
public_methods.signature: build_circuit(self, num_time_qubits: int, num_asset_qubits: int, num_layers: int) -> QuantumCircuit
public_methods.returns: outward/inward snake-layer CRCA circuit for R_v

public_methods.name: conditional_probability
public_methods.signature: conditional_probability(self, params: np.ndarray, index_i: int, index_j: tuple) -> float
public_methods.returns: F_phi(i,j), the exposure-ancilla |1> probability

public_methods.name: mse_loss
public_methods.signature: mse_loss(self, params: np.ndarray, target_fn: np.ndarray) -> float
public_methods.returns: scalar MSE loss L_v(phi)

module_hierarchy.description: Assembles G_theta, R_v, R_p, R_q into the full CVA encoding unitary A_Theta and its Grover iterate Q, and exposes the marked-subspace projector

public_methods.name: assemble
public_methods.signature: assemble(self, g_theta: QuantumCircuit, r_v: QuantumCircuit, r_p: QuantumCircuit, r_q: QuantumCircuit) -> QuantumCircuit
public_methods.returns: composed A_Theta = R_q R_p R_v G_theta circuit

public_methods.name: marked_amplitude_statevector
public_methods.signature: marked_amplitude_statevector(self, circuit: QuantumCircuit) -> float
public_methods.returns: exact a_CVA via statevector simulation, projecting onto |111> on ancillas

public_methods.name: grover_iterate
public_methods.signature: grover_iterate(self, circuit: QuantumCircuit) -> QuantumCircuit
public_methods.returns: Q = -A S0 A^dagger Sf circuit

public_methods.name: amplified_circuit
public_methods.signature: amplified_circuit(self, circuit: QuantumCircuit, k: int) -> QuantumCircuit
public_methods.returns: Q^k A circuit for Grover power k

module_hierarchy.description: Contrast-aware Bayesian Iterative Quantum Amplitude Estimation (CABIQAE) — the paper's core algorithmic contribution

public_methods.name: estimate
public_methods.signature: estimate(self, circuit_executor: Callable[[int, int], int], epsilon: float, alpha: float = 0.1, n_batch: int = 256) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: (a_hat, a_lower, a_upper) point estimate and credible interval

public_methods.name: bayesian_update
public_methods.signature: bayesian_update(self, alpha0: float, beta0: float, n_shots: int, n_success: int) -> Tuple[float, float]
public_methods.returns: (alpha_post, beta_post) Beta posterior parameters

public_methods.name: compute_cri
public_methods.signature: compute_cri(self, interval: Tuple[float, float], k: int, alpha_post: float, beta_post: float, alpha: float) -> Tuple[Tuple[float,float], Tuple[float,float], np.ndarray]
public_methods.returns: (new_angle_interval, amplitude_interval, posterior_grid)

public_methods.name: find_next_k
public_methods.signature: find_next_k(self, interval: Tuple[float,float], current_k: int, tau_c: float, rho_min: float, posterior: np.ndarray) -> int
public_methods.returns: next Grover power k maximising posterior-averaged Fisher information per query

public_methods.name: prepare_prior
public_methods.signature: prepare_prior(self, posterior: np.ndarray, next_k: int, n_prior_samples: int = 2000) -> Tuple[float, float]
public_methods.returns: (alpha0, beta0) transported Beta prior for the new Grover power

public_methods.name: contrast_model
public_methods.signature: contrast_model(self, k: int, c0: float, tau_c: float) -> float
public_methods.returns: c(k) = c0 * exp(-(2k+1)/tau_c)

public_methods.name: estimate
public_methods.signature: estimate(self, circuit_executor: Callable[[int, int], int], epsilon: float, alpha: float = 0.1, n_batch: int = 256) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: noise-naive Beta-BIQAE estimate (ideal sin^2(K*theta) likelihood, no contrast decay)

public_methods.name: estimate
public_methods.signature: estimate(self, circuit_executor: Callable[[int, int], int], epsilon: float, n_particles: int = 300) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: Bayesian Amplitude Estimation (Ramôa & Santos 2025) estimate via sequential Monte Carlo posterior

public_methods.name: estimate
public_methods.signature: estimate(self, circuit_executor: Callable[[int, int], int], n_shots: int) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: unamplified Bernoulli-sampling estimate (k=0 baseline)

module_hierarchy.description: Hardware calibration of the exponential contrast-decay model (c0, tau_c, b) and readout-error mitigation, used to build the hardware-replay model

public_methods.name: fit_contrast_model
public_methods.signature: fit_contrast_model(self, k_values: np.ndarray, observed_probs: np.ndarray, ideal_theta: float, b: float) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: (c0, tau_c, r_squared) fitted from log(c_k) = beta0 + beta1*K

public_methods.name: readout_mitigate
public_methods.signature: readout_mitigate(self, raw_prob: float, r0: float, r1: float) -> float
public_methods.returns: affine-corrected probability p_mit

public_methods.name: build_hardware_replay_model
public_methods.signature: build_hardware_replay_model(self, hardware_probs: Dict[int, float]) -> Callable[[int, int], int]
public_methods.returns: a circuit_executor callable that replays Binomial(n_shots, p_hw[k]) draws for CABIQAE/BIQAE/BAE

module_hierarchy.description: Classical risk-neutral multi-asset Monte Carlo CVA benchmark, finite-grid projection, CDS bootstrap and Black-Scholes-Merton pricing

public_methods.name: simulate_paths
public_methods.signature: simulate_paths(self, n_paths: int, monitoring_dates: np.ndarray, seed: int = 100000) -> np.ndarray
public_methods.returns: simulated correlated log-normal price paths [n_paths, n_dates, n_assets]

public_methods.name: call_price
public_methods.signature: call_price(self, S: float, K: float, T: float, r: float, q: float, sigma: float) -> float
public_methods.returns: European call price c(t,T,K)

public_methods.name: put_price
public_methods.signature: put_price(self, S: float, K: float, T: float, r: float, q: float, sigma: float) -> float
public_methods.returns: European put price p(t,T,K)

public_methods.name: bootstrap_survival_curve
public_methods.signature: bootstrap_survival_curve(self, cds_tenors: np.ndarray, cds_spreads: np.ndarray, recovery_cds: float, discount_curve: Callable) -> Callable
public_methods.returns: piecewise-constant survival probability function P(0,u)

public_methods.name: monte_carlo_cva
public_methods.signature: monte_carlo_cva(self, paths: np.ndarray, netting_set: List[dict], survival_fn: Callable, discount_fn: Callable, recovery_cva: float) -> Tuple[float, float]
public_methods.returns: (CVA_cont_MC estimate, standard error)

public_methods.name: finite_grid_cva
public_methods.signature: finite_grid_cva(self, prob_tensor: np.ndarray, exposure_tensor: np.ndarray, discount_vec: np.ndarray, default_incr_vec: np.ndarray, recovery_cva: float) -> float
public_methods.returns: CVA_tab_Delta tabulated finite-grid benchmark

module_hierarchy.description: Constructs the finite time-market grid (bin edges, joint probability tensor P_{i,j}, positive exposure tensor V+_{i,j}) that both the classical benchmark and the quantum circuit consume

public_methods.name: truncate_domain
public_methods.signature: truncate_domain(self, mu: float, sigma: float, n_std: float = 3.0) -> Tuple[float, float]
public_methods.returns: truncated price domain D_k = [max(mu-n_std*sigma,0), mu+n_std*sigma]

public_methods.name: build_probability_tensor
public_methods.signature: build_probability_tensor(self, paths: np.ndarray, bin_edges: List[np.ndarray], monitoring_dates: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray
public_methods.returns: joint probability tensor P_{i,j}, shape [M, N1, ..., Nd]

public_methods.name: build_exposure_tensor
public_methods.signature: build_exposure_tensor(self, netting_set: List[dict], bin_edges: List[np.ndarray], monitoring_dates: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray
public_methods.returns: positive exposure tensor V+_{i,j}

public_methods.name: rescale_constants
public_methods.signature: rescale_constants(self, exposure_tensor: np.ndarray, discount_vec: np.ndarray, default_incr_vec: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: (C_v, C_p, C_q) rescaling constants so all encoded quantities lie in [0,1]

module_hierarchy.description: Standalone loss functions matching the SIR mathematical_spec (cross-entropy, MSE, KL diagnostic)
Formal end-to-end error-budget decomposition (finite-grid, encoding, amplitude-estimation contributions) reproducing Section 4.5

public_methods.name: compute_budget
public_methods.signature: compute_budget(self, cva_cont_mc: float, cva_tab: float, cva_sv: float, cva_ae: float) -> Dict[str, float]
public_methods.returns: dict with eps_grid, eps_enc, eps_ae, alpha_n, beta_theta, and total propagated bound

module_hierarchy.description: Trajectory-level metrics: median relative error, query-cost accounting (N_q), coverage, log-log scaling exponent fits

public_methods.name: query_cost
public_methods.signature: query_cost(self, stages: List[Tuple[int,int]]) -> int
public_methods.returns: N_q = sum(n_s * (2*k_s+1)) accumulated oracle queries

public_methods.name: fit_scaling_exponent
public_methods.signature: fit_scaling_exponent(self, n_q_values: np.ndarray, errors: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[float, float, float]
public_methods.returns: (beta, alpha, r_squared) from log(error) = alpha + beta*log(N_q)

module_hierarchy.description: YAML config loading and global random-seed management

tensor_flows.name: CVA oracle forward construction (state prep -> encoding -> readout)

input.shape: [512] complex amplitudes (9 qubits: 2 time + 4 asset + 3 ancilla)

steps: x = |0>^{⊗(m+n)}  → [2 time qubits, 4 asset qubits] all-zero register
x = G_theta(x)  → entangled joint time-market superposition sum_ij sqrt(P_ij)|i,j>, shape unchanged (64 basis states over 6 qubits)
x = R_v(x)  → entangles exposure ancilla a_v: sqrt(1-v~_ij)|0>+sqrt(v~_ij)|1>, conditioned on full (i,j)
x = R_p(x)  → entangles discount ancilla a_p, conditioned on time register i only
x = R_q(x)  → entangles default ancilla a_q, conditioned on time register i only
xi = x  → full 9-qubit state |xi>, 512 complex amplitudes
a_CVA = <xi| Pi_111 |xi> = ||Pi_111|xi>||^2  → project onto (a_v,a_p,a_q)=|111>, sum probability mass
CVA_Delta = M(1-R_CVA) * C_v * C_p * C_q * a_CVA  → rescale to monetary units

tensor_flows.name: CABIQAE adaptive estimation loop
steps: loop: run Q^{k_t}A for n_batch shots via circuit_executor  → (N_t, Y_t) cumulative counts
posterior = bayesian_update(alpha_0,t, beta_0,t, N_t, Y_t)  → Beta(alpha_post, beta_post) over observed probability p_t
(new_interval, [a_l,a_u], pi_t) = compute_cri(I_t, k_t, posterior, alpha)  → pull posterior back through p_k(theta)=b+c(k)(sin^2(K theta)-b) to latent angle, take equal-tailed credible interval
if a_u - a_l <= 2*epsilon: return (a_l+a_u)/2, a_l, a_u  → STOP
k_next = find_next_k(new_interval, k_t, tau_c, rho_min, pi_t)  → maximise posterior-Fisher-information score per query subject to identifiability + contrast-scale constraints
if k_next > k_t: (alpha_0, beta_0) = prepare_prior(pi_t, k_next, N_prior)  → transport posterior samples through new likelihood, moment-match to Beta, reset (N,Y)=(0,0)
else: continue sampling at same k_t with updated interval
repeat until convergence

tensor_flows.name: Finite-grid classical benchmark pipeline

input.name: market_data (equity closes, vol surfaces, OIS curve, CDS spreads)

output.name: (CVA_cont_MC, CVA_tab_Delta, P_ij, V+_ij, discount_vec, default_incr_vec, C_v, C_p, C_q)

steps: calibrate piecewise-constant volatility buckets from ATM implied-vol surface  → sigma_k,j per underlying/bucket
bootstrap survival curve from CDS par spreads (Eq. 26)  → P(0,u) deterministic curve
simulate N_MC=2e5 correlated log-normal paths (Eq. 9/11) with Cholesky-factorised correlation  → paths[N_MC, M, d]
price netting-set instruments at each monitoring date via Black-Scholes-Merton (Eq. 13-15)  → V(t) per path/date
compute CVA_cont_MC via Eq. 30 (continuous-underlying Monte Carlo estimator)
truncate price domains D_k (mu +/- 3 sigma) and bin into N_k=4 bins per asset  → bin_edges
project simulated paths onto finite grid  → empirical conditional bin probabilities Q_D(S_ti in B_j)
build joint probability tensor P_ij (Eq. 32) and positive exposure tensor V+_ij
compute CVA_tab_Delta via Eq. 31 (finite-grid tabulated benchmark) for comparison against the quantum circuit

model.qcbm_layers_L: 6  # ASSUMED confidence 0.95 from Table 5, but final selection criterion for L was qualitative (see SIR ambiguity #3)
model.numerical_floor_eps_num: 1e-8  # ASSUMED: paper states epsilon_num > 0 small constant but does not give exact value; standard log-floor default used

training.optimizer: adam  # ASSUMED confidence 0.4 (SIR training_pipeline confidence=0.5): paper does not name the optimizer; Adam is the common default for QCBM/VQE gradient training
training.learning_rate: 0.05  # ASSUMED: not stated in paper, common QCBM starting LR
training.qcbm_iterations: 1100  # ASSUMED, read off Figure 8/9 training-curve x-axis, not stated numerically
training.seed: 100000  # matches the one disclosed seed (classical MC benchmark, NMC draws); quantum-training seed not stated, reused for consistency, confidence 0.5

data.market_data_source: LSEG Workspace (proprietary) -- not redistributable; download.sh documents manual retrieval steps and provides a synthetic fallback

evaluation.biqae_target_half_width_cva: 1e-3  # deliberately tighter than CABIQAE per paper's stated rationale (Table 10 footnote)

hardware.backend_name: ibm_basquecountry  # requires IBM Quantum + Basque Quantum (BasQ) access; falls back to AerSimulator/Statevector when no credentials are configured
hardware.use_qctrl_performance_management: true  # requires separate Q-CTRL / IBM Quantum Function credentials; ASSUMED optional flag since internal workflow is a documented black box (SIR confidence 0.3)

runtime.reason: Core quantum-circuit construction, statevector/Aer simulation, transpilation
Noisy and noiseless local simulation backend
Submitting circuits / Qiskit Functions to IBM Quantum cloud backends (ibm_basquecountry)
Numerical arrays, tensor construction, linear algebra (Cholesky factorisation)
Optimisation routines (classical parameter training), Beta-distribution utilities, statistical fits
Autograd-based gradient optimisation for QCBM/CRCA variational training (parameter-shift gradients wrapped as PyTorch tensors)
Market-data tabular handling (equity closes, vol surfaces, CDS curves)
Reproducing paper figures (KL trajectories, error-vs-query curves, contrast calibration plots)

entrypoints.purpose: Train QCBM (G_theta) and CRCA (R_v, R_p, R_q) variational parameters against the finite-grid classical benchmark

cli_args.help: Which block to train: qcbm | crca_rp | crca_rq | crca_rv | all

entrypoints.purpose: Run the amplitude-estimation comparison (CABIQAE vs BIQAE vs BAE vs DCS) in noiseless or hardware-replay regime and reproduce the error-budget decomposition

cli_args.help: Number of independent estimation trajectories R
Directory to write result CSV/JSON and figures

entrypoints.purpose: Single-run: build the trained CVA oracle, run one CABIQAE estimation trajectory, and print the recovered monetary CVA value
Paper-specific script: execute the Grover-power calibration sweep on real IBM Quantum hardware (or a saved calibration file) and fit the exponential contrast-decay model (c0, tau_c, b)

cli_args.help: validation | cva -- which circuit family to calibrate
Where to save fitted contrast parameters

risk_assessment.description: The internal transpilation, layout, routing and error-suppression logic of Q-CTRL's Performance Management Qiskit Function is proprietary and not disclosed, so the hardware-replay execution path (BackendManager.run_with_qctrl) cannot be reproduced exactly -- only its documented external interface (submit abstract circuit, receive mitigated counts) can be implemented.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement BackendManager.run_with_qctrl as a thin wrapper around the real Q-CTRL Qiskit Function when credentials are available, with a clearly-labelled fallback that replays the calibration data reported in Table 17/18 of the paper (contrast-decay parameters c0, tau_c, b) via ContrastCalibrator.build_hardware_replay_model, so the estimator comparison remains reproducible without live hardware access.

risk_assessment.description: Optimizer, learning rate, and exact iteration counts for QCBM/CRCA training are not stated in the paper (SIR training_pipeline confidence = 0.5); a wrong choice could change trained-circuit fidelity and downstream CVA_SV(Theta) value materially.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose optimizer/lr/iterations as config.yaml fields with ASSUMED comments (done above); design VariationalTrainer.train_qcbm/train_crca to accept any scipy.optimize- or torch.optim-compatible optimizer via a config string, so a user can swap in the exact optimizer once/if the authors' code repository (Borràs, 2026, github.com/gborras10/Quantum-CVA) is consulted.

risk_assessment.description: Market data (LSEG Workspace) is proprietary and not redistributable, so the classical CVA benchmark and finite-grid construction cannot be run out-of-the-box without the user's own LSEG access.
risk_assessment.mitigation: data/download.sh documents the required LSEG pulls (equity closes, ATM vol surfaces, EUR OIS curve, Iberdrola CDS spreads) and provides a small synthetic market-data generator (matching Table 4's calibrated bin edges/discount factors/default increments) so the pipeline is runnable end-to-end without live LSEG credentials.

risk_assessment.description: The exposure rotation R_v ansatz ('outward/inward snake' topology, Figure 5) is described precisely only via circuit diagrams for a single variational layer (L=1); generalising to L=2 (the value actually used) requires inferring the layer-repetition convention.
risk_assessment.source: SIR architecture.modules R_v, confidence 0.9, but layer-composition rule only stated qualitatively ('composing several repetitions... with independent parameters in each repetition')
risk_assessment.mitigation: SnakeCRCA.build_circuit takes num_layers explicitly and repeats the depicted L=1 block with fresh parameters each repetition, matching the paper's stated (if not fully diagrammed) composition rule; unit-test against the reported logical resource counts in Table 5 (98 single-qubit gates, 86 CNOT-equivalent two-qubit gates for L=2) to validate the inferred repetition scheme.

risk_assessment.description: Random seeds for quantum-circuit statevector/finite-shot sampling and for CABIQAE/BIQAE/BAE trajectory generation are not documented (only the classical Monte Carlo benchmark seed, 1e5, is given).
risk_assessment.mitigation: utils/config.py centralises a single global seed (default reused from the classical benchmark, 100000) applied consistently to numpy, torch and Qiskit's simulator RNGs, so results are at least internally reproducible run-to-run even if they cannot be guaranteed bit-identical to the paper's own runs.

risk_assessment.description: Numerical instability risk in CABIQAE's ComputeCRI/FindNextK when the posterior places mass near a branch boundary of sin^2(K*theta), or when c(k) decays enough that pk(theta) approaches the baseline b (Fisher information -> 0), potentially causing division-by-zero in Eq. 64/70.
risk_assessment.source: SIR mathematical_spec 'Posterior-averaged Fisher-information scheduling score', Appendix A.3 numerical-implementation notes
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement the paper's own stabilisation: clip probabilities to [delta, 1-delta] before computing Fisher information (Eq. 70), and fall back to a uniform density / weakly-informative Beta prior when the posterior grid normalisation Z_t is degenerate, exactly as specified in the SIR's Appendix A.3 numerical-implementation notes.
